name: Orange and Coffee Bean Cordial
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the orange and make as many small slits in it as possible.
2. Stick a coffee bean in each slit.
3. Place the remaining coffee beans in the jar with the orange slices.
4. Add the sugar cubes and chopped vanilla bean to the jar.
5. Pour Cognac (or vodka or rum) over the ingredients until the mixture is completely covered.
6. Allow the cordial to mature for 1½ to 2 months, storing it in a cool, dark place.
7. After the maturation period, strain the contents through a fine mesh, pressing on the solids to extract all the juice and flavor from the orange slices.
8. Serve chilled.
Vodka or rum may also be used.
Yield: Approximately 1 pint of cordial
Container: A wide-mouth quart jar
